heading: National Institute on Deafness and Other Communication Disorders Advisory Board
label: 285m–4 National Institute on Deafness and Other Communication Disorders Advisory Board

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish in the Institute the National Deafness and Other Communication Disorders Advisory Board (hereafter in this section referred to as the “Advisory Board”).

children.heading: Composition; qualifications; appointed and ex officio members
children.label: b Composition; qualifications; appointed and ex officio members
A
children.text: twelve members from individuals who are scientists, physicians, and other health and rehabilitation professionals, who are not officers or employees of the United States, and who represent the specialties and disciplines relevant to deafness and other communication disorders, including not less than two persons with a communication disorder; and

children.label: B
children.text: six members from the general public who are knowledgeable with respect to such disorders, including not less than one person with a communication disorder and not less than one person who is a parent of an individual with such a disorder.

children.label: A
children.text: The Assistant Secretary for Health, the Director of NIH, the Director of the National Institute on Deafness and Other Communication Disorders, the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, the Under Secretary for Health of the Department of Veterans Affairs, and the Assistant Secretary of Defense for Health Affairs (or the designees of such officers).

children.label: B
children.text: Such other officers and employees of the United States as the Secretary determines necessary for the Advisory Board to carry out its functions.

children.heading: Compensation
children.label: c Compensation
children.text: Members of an Advisory Board who are officers or employees of the Federal Government shall serve as members of the Advisory Board without compensation in addition to that received in their regular public employment. Other members of the Board shall receive compensation at rates not to exceed the daily equivalent of the annual rate in effect for grade GS–18 of the General Schedule for each day (including traveltime) they are engaged in the performance of their duties as members of the Board.

children.heading: Term of office; vacancies
children.label: d Term of office; vacancies
children.text: The term of office of an appointed member of the Advisory Board is four years, except that no term of office may extend beyond the expiration of the Advisory Board. Any member appointed to fill a vacancy for an unexpired term shall be appointed for the remainder of such term. A member may serve after the expiration of the member’s term until a successor has taken office. If a vacancy occurs in the Advisory Board, the Secretary shall make an appointment to fill the vacancy not later than 90 days from the date the vacancy occurred.

children.heading: Chairman
children.label: e Chairman
children.text: The members of the Advisory Board shall select a chairman from among the appointed members.

children.heading: Personnel; executive director; professional and clerical staff members; consultants; information and administrative support services and facilities
children.label: f Personnel; executive director; professional and clerical staff members; consultants; information and administrative support services and facilities
children.text: The Secretary shall, after consultation with and consideration of the recommendations of the Advisory Board, provide the Advisory Board with an executive director and one other professional staff member. In addition, the Secretary shall, after consultation with and consideration of the recommendations of the Advisory Board, provide the Advisory Board with such additional professional staff members, such clerical staff members, such services of consultants, such information, and (through contracts or other arrangements) such administrative support services and facilities, as the Secretary determines are necessary for the Advisory Board to carry out its functions.

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: g Meetings
children.text: The Advisory Board shall meet at the call of the chairman or upon request of the Director of the Institute, but not less often than four times a year.
review and evaluate the implementation of the plan prepared under and periodically update the plan to ensure its continuing relevance;section 285m–1(a) of this title

for the purpose of assuring the most effective use and organization of resources respecting deafness and other communication disorders, advise and make recommendations to the Congress, the Secretary, the Director of NIH, the Director of the Institute, and the heads of other appropriate Federal agencies for the implementation and revision of such plan; and

maintain liaison with other advisory bodies related to Federal agencies involved in the implementation of such plan and with key non-Federal entities involved in activities affecting the control of such disorders.

children.heading: Subcommittee activities; workshops and conferences; collection of data
children.label: i Subcommittee activities; workshops and conferences; collection of data
children.text: In carrying out its functions, the Advisory Board may establish subcommittees, convene workshops and conferences, and collect data. Such subcommittees may be composed of Advisory Board members and nonmember consultants with expertise in the particular area addressed by such subcommittees. The subcommittees may hold such meetings as are necessary to enable them to carry out their activities.

children.heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 109–482, title I, § 104(b)(1)(J)120 Stat. 3693Jan. 15, 2007
children.label: j Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 109–482, title I, § 104(b)(1)(J)120 Stat. 3693Jan. 15, 2007

children.heading: Commencement of existence
children.label: k Commencement of existence
children.text: The National Deafness and Other Communication Disorders Advisory Board shall be established not later than .April 1, 1989
